div.div:
Sensor Inputs
CAMERA, LiDAR & IMU
Ground Truth inputs
CAN analyzer - Selects IMU data and other positional data from CAN feed at `/received_messages` and analyzes/publishes it.

display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 698px; height: 1px; padding-top: 550px; margin-left: 1125px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.div: - xacro files for generation the car based on the car specific parameters (`urdf/vehicle/`)
- a gazebo plugin for a velodyne puck lidar in the simulation (`gazebo_plugin`). For further information checkout the source code
(rosyard_description) This is the package for supplementary gazebo/simulation/model stuff. It contains:
- xacro files for generation the car based on the car specific parameters (`urdf/vehicle/`)
- a gazebo plugin for a velodyne puck lidar in the simulation (`gazebo_plugin`). For further information checkout the source code
- a gazebo plugin to handle the simple physics simulation (`gazebo_race_car_model/`)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 848px; height: 1px; padding-top: 541px; margin-left: 1872px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
(#rosyard_gazebo) This package for gazebo specific files contains:
- the simulation launch file (`launch/simulation.launch`) - c++ nodes (`src/`)
- the different tracks as world files (`worlds/`)
(#rosyard_nano_tools) This package for controlling the jetson nano contains:
- different python nodes and scripts (`scripts/`)
- (See Janek's master project as this package was created during it)
(rqt_fssim_track_editor_copy) This package is copy from AMZ and contains:
- a rqt plugin for generating new tracks, buggy
(track_conversion) This folder contains no ros package and contains:
- a conversion script for the yaml files from rqt_fssim_track_editor_copy into gazebo worlds, in the default case expects a project structure like this wiki
discribes (`convert_yaml_to_worlds.py`)

switch.text: (#rosyard_gazebo) This package for gazebo specific files contains:...

div.div: (#rosyard_detection) This package for the cone detection contains:
- YOLOv3 components (`scripts/model_data`,`scripts/yolo3`) [see here for more information]
- QA initialization ( change to TRUE in \rosyard\src\rosyard_common\config\general_config.yaml )

switch.text: (#rosyard_detection) This package for the cone detection contains:...

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 858px; height: 1px; padding-top: 861px; margin-left: 1867px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
# Publishing
detection_camera_topic_name: /detection/camera # message type: sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 (x,y,z,color) color is one float value : detection_camera_rate: 30 # [Hz] detection_lidar_topic_name: /detection/lidar # message type: sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 (x,y,z,color) color is one float value : detection_lidar_rate: 30 # [Hz]
# Debug Publishing
detection_camera_debug_topic_name: /detection/debug/camera # message type: Image
detection_lidar_debug_topic_name: /detection/debug/lidar # message type: Image
# Nodes to use
detection_camera_node: node_cheat_cone_detection_camera.py # name of the launch file [node_cheat_cone_detection_camera.py, node_yolo_cone_detection_camera.py,]
detection_lidar_node: node_cheat_cone_detection_lidar.py # name of the launch file [node_cheat_cone_detection_lidar.py, node_clustering_cone_detection_lidar.py]
# QA
detection_camera_qa : node_qa_cone_detection_camera_general.py # name of the used node [node_qa_cone_detection_camera_general.py] detection_lidar_qa : node_qa_cone_detection_lidar_general.py # name of the used node [node_qa_cone_detection_lidar_general.py]

div.span: The purpose of nodes implementing this template is doing SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping). This node takes cone detections. It creates a virtual map and a localization of the car in this map.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 538px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1206px; margin-left: 1132px;

div.div:
(#rosyard_slam) This package for the slam implementations contains:
- c++ nodes (`include/` and `src/`)
- python nodes (`scripts/`)

Further more it adds a centerline.

switch.text: (#rosyard_control) This package for high level driver contains:...

div.div: driving_suggestion_topic_name: /driving/command_suggestion # message type: rosyard_common/ControlCommand, suggested control command
driving_approved_topic_name: /driving/command_approved # message type: rosyard_common/ControlCommand, approved control command which depends on emergency control
emergency_msg_topic_name: /emergency # message type: rosyard_common/EmergencyControl, kill command can published to this topic by hand to stop car
driver_node: cheat_driver.py # name of the launch file [manual_node, center_line_driver, cheat_driver.py] cheat_driver only works well if you set cheating_command in node_parameters
driving_status_control: driving_status_control #name of the launch file [driving_status_control]
